subject: Physics
title: Hubble's Expanding Universe
description: A quick, practical guide to Hubble's Expanding Universe.

hook.text: In 1929, Edwin Hubble proved that galaxies are flying apart—the universe itself is expanding.

buildup.text: Hubble measured light from distant galaxies and found it was redshifted—stretched to longer wavelengths.

discovery.text: The farther away a galaxy, the faster it recedes. This means the universe is expanding in every direction.

twist.text: If everything is flying apart now, rewinding time means everything was once together—evidence for the Big Bang.

climax.text: Hubble's discovery overturned Einstein's static universe. Even Einstein called his own cosmological constant his biggest blunder.

punchline.text: Galaxies fleeing in all directions. Run the film backward and you get the Big Bang.

quiz.question: What did Hubble's redshift observations prove?
options: Light slows down over long distances
